messages.text: Hello good afternoon. My name is [Patient] and I am trying to look for a private based endometriosis specialist in Spain.
A checklist would be helpful.
There’s lots of specialists in Barcelona but its important to find the right one.

So I have had surgery already back in the USA. It was in December of 2021 and I had some relief afterwards but the symptoms have progressively gotten worse. I’ve done an MRI and also had a new IUD Morena inserted. Since then I also started a medication called Ryego that’s put my body into menopause and things are worse than ever.
I keep experiencing hip pain that goes down to my legs and I can’t walk and also rectal pain. I am about to lose my job because of missing work.
The anti-inflammatories they give me basically aren’t working.
I’ve changed my diet and that only helps to an extent.
My current specialist is putting a lot of pressure for me to get pregnant keeps insisting on just using ant-inflammatory pills.
The MRI was after my surgery but it showed that the endometriosis was where my appendix was. They removed it during my surgery due to adhesions.
The surgeon was an endometriosis specialist that did a robotic surgery. He’s since retired.
So has the specialist that ordered the MRI. The current specialist claims she has no idea where it is in my body but also has not asked for a new MRI.

My current specialist refuses to do another surgery until I’ve had all the children I wish to have. At this point, I am not comfortable with a pregnancy due to the pain and the possibility of still being in pain afterwards.

It goes down to the thigh but no farther than that. I don’t think I’ve felt numbness or anything like that. I feel urgency with bowel movements or if I am holding in urine. Sometimes that causes discomfort. 
As far as I know of the scan was specifically for endometriosis. 
At this point I want to prioritise pain relief and just have a quality of life.
Fertility is important as well but this is to the point where it’s really affecting my daily life.
It also causes headaches that almost feel like a migraine. I didn’t know that was a symptom till recently.

Thank you so much for the information. I don’t have the names of the specialist at the public hospital but I do have a name of a specialist that I have a video call with on Friday. She’s based in Barcelona.
Dra. María Eulalia Fernández Montolí
She has 20 years of experience with endometriosis supposedly and allowed video appointments. I thought that would be best for a first appointment before we go in person.
Also, I agree either way all of this checklist. I’m a bit concerned that this is potentially near my bowel or rectal area and possibly the bladder.
Ok thank you for the help. I’ll keep this in mind and keep on looking depending on her answers.
Hi, I saw you via Amy and was hoping you could help me find the right specialist for my endometriosis and adenomiosis, I live in Portugal

I have deep and infiltrating endometriosis level IV and adenomiosis. 

I live in Viseu, Portugal. I okay with traveling max to Lisbon 

I have insurance from my company that give me Access to discount on CUF hospitals
Hi, I'd like help finding a specialist. Ideally a pelvic physiotherapist or pelvic osteopath with experience in endometriosis care in the Woking, Surrey, UK area

Pelvic pain, bloating, constipation, pain with a full bladder and pain after emptying my bowels. 

Diagnosed with endometriosis in November 2023, and diagnosed with PCOS in 2021. I have had 5 sessions of pelvic osteopathy whilst I was in Australia which helped massively, but symptoms have since worsened/returned

Knaphill, Woking. Prefer to be seen in surrounding areas nearby. 

I would be self funding.

I'm looking for a practitioner who is able to offer internal treatment.

I did briefly see a pelvic osteo in Guildford and I felt that the hands on time was not enough for the price of the sessions. 

Ongoing management
*treatment to support pelvic floor function
Please let me know if you need any further information

Ivd endometriosis on my bladder  bowl and womb, its recommended i have a hysterectomy.
My area is newcastle upon tyne
Ive already had 1 overy removed and both tubes
I'd travel however would prefer near home
Im interested in private

Ive not had a mri however ive had ultrasound internal and external. 
Ive had surgery twice i don't think it was a endo specialist as they said they could remove the lesions
Couldn't
Pain, generally unwell
I have the marina coil too to help with the bleeding
Its spreading from last surgery so consultant said the hysterectomy would be needed to stop it.

I think it was dec last yr
Cramps sharp pain. Having to empty bladder all the time. And after I eat needing to use toilet strsightbaway
Hi

Hi, my condition is already confirmed endometriosis. 
I had a diagnostic lap in 2017 which confirmed it and a large endometrioma drained. I had a further surgery in 2018 to excise fhe endometrioma and since then have been relatively pain free. I have recently been dealing with pain again and am currently on a long waiting list to be seen which is 12 months wait. 
I am located in Dy6 and I am open to travel. 
I have insurance with my work provider but they put menstrual health on my exclusions because I was having trouble at the time. So it would be self funding. 
Ideally I need nhs and want to see if I have other options aside from Birmingham women’s where I am currently waiting. 
Thank you

Thank you. 
I get left sided stabbing pain on and off on my left side which I believe could be from having a large endometrioma removed and my ovary now being stuck by my pouch of Douglas. 
I get on and off sharp pelvic pain at times that is relieved form bowel movements but this flares up every now and then. 
But my main concern is since my period end of November I have been having daily pain in my coccyx, rectum, vagina. It feels like heavy pain, aching, stiffness, tightness and pressure. This varies day to day and I find it hard to sit down. 
It intensifies during my period. And can differ day to day but is always there. 
I tend to take paracetamol mostly as I can’t have Ibupophen. The doctor has prescribed codeine and amityptaline but I haven’t yet taken these. 
I have been referred to Birmingham women’s and have had a lumbar mri which they are repeating in a couple of weeks. I’ve had a colposcopy which came back clear and an internal ultrasound which showed my ovary being stuck. 
Thank you
Hi

Both pain management and quality of life.

I have health insurance however I am unsure if they will cover fertility treatments but that is something I can look at, so I would like to see the self funding options to work out costs.
Dr Anthony Griffis actually has done both my surgeries prior and I have considered using him again so the fact he’s recommended for both is very reassuring!
Hello I am looking to find a endometriosis specialist in Cardiff
Endometriosis and Adenomysis. Progressively getting worse. Recent internal ultrasound and Mri.
Private cover

Hi there,

This is Jack from DocMap. Thanks for sharing a bit about your situation. It's helpful to know you've recently had an internal ultrasound and MRI.

Just a few quick questions so we can match you to the right specialist:

* Has your endometriosis/adenomyosis been formally diagnosed by a specialist, and has any stage or severity been mentioned?
*Yes no stage been mentioned 

* Are your symptoms mainly pelvic pain, heavy bleeding, bowel-related, bladder-related, or a mix?

Mixture 

* Have you had any previous surgery for endometriosis, or are you currently on any treatment (e.g. hormonal therapy, coil, medication)?

No surgery currently taking Ryeqo for treatment 
* Which insurer are you with?
* AXA

Once we have this, we can narrow this down properly for you 👍
Tiredness
I just need recommendations for Cardiff
All

I’m not really sure where to start, as there’s a lot to explain. I have had endometriosis since I was 11, but I was only diagnosed with stage 4 around two years ago. I am now 23.
Over the years, I’ve been to the doctors many times because the pain has constantly increased. I’ve also been in and out of A&E, but I’m often left waiting for hours. Because of how severe the pain is, sitting in a waiting room chair becomes unbearable, and I usually end up discharging myself.
I was previously on a waiting list to see a gynaecologist. I was seen once, but then I was wrongly discharged, as they said I seemed to be managing okay. I have now been placed back on the waiting list to see a gynaecologist again.
What I really want is to be seen by a gynaecologist or, ideally, a specialist in endometriosis. I know my condition has worsened since my diagnosis, based on an MRI scan. I would really like to have a laparoscopy so that the next step could be excision surgery.
I have also been to the doctor several times because my bladder often feels swollen and sore, and I experience extremely painful bowel movements. I’m based in Coventry happy to travel as far as London if absolutely necessary. My budget is around £200, please let me know if you need anymore information? Thank you

What I really want is to be seen by a gynaecologist or, ideally, a specialist in endometriosis. I know my condition has worsened since my diagnosis, based on an MRI scan. I would really like to have a laparoscopy so that the next step could be excision surgery.

Thank you. I have been told mixed responses from doctors over the past seven years and I’m not sure exactly what’s wrong with me. I have severe period pain occasionally, when trying to go to the toilet or when my abdomen is inflamed etc. I am often sick and faint during these episodes. Ultrasound scans have told me mixed messages - I have an 8cm endometrium, then that I have a swollen fallopian tube and that one of my ovaries is dead, then that it’s neither of those things and that it’s an area of trapped fluid from adhesions and that I have a small amount of endo on my bowel. Who do you recommend I see? I am based in London, NHS only and willing to travel or for trials etc. I am currently on dianogest which controls symptoms well but still there I can feel it! Thank you

Please can I ask where you got these surgeons from?
That these are the 4 in particular that have been provided?

Because from the list you gave, there doesn’t seem to be many of the surgeons in london. Such as Mr Hemant Vakharia, who has a lot more reviews than some of the surgeons you gave. He is also listed as an endo specialist at a bsge accredited centre. 
In fact a couple of the private options you gave, do not have a private bsge accredited centre and so in the UK I should not be having surgery at any private centre that isn’t bsge accredited because I have stage 4. The list you gave, a couple of them only have NHS bsge centre
That would be feedback

All the accredited centres are on the bsge website, this includes both NHS and private centres 
This is the list of provisional centres:
https://www.bsge.org.uk/centre/category/provisional-centres/
This is the list of centres:
https://www.bsge.org.uk/centre/category/accredited-centres/

Yes please do that, because usually in the UK, if you have stage 4 it must be at a BSGE accredited centre, including privately. And not just a bsge accredited surgeon who has a accredited centre on the NHS

Mr Hemant Vakharia does very complex cases including thoracic cases. 
I think the reason your search didn’t pull him is because it doesn’t show that he works at the London Clinic. A very good private hospital. If you look at the provisional bsge centre list I gave you, his colorectal surgeon who he works with for stage 4 cases is renowned Mr Ian Jenkins. Mr Vakharia also has a private MDT as well. 
https://www.thelondonclinic.co.uk/experts/mr-hemant-vakharia
https://www.finder.bupa.co.uk/Consultant/view/246568/mr_hemant_vakharia
https://www.doctify.com/uk/specialist/hemant-vakharia
In addition, certain patient forums, such as Nancy Nook, do not allow certain negative reviews of doctors. The “list” they have is not validated in the UK, there is no vetting process
https://www.thelondonclinic.co.uk/experts/mr-ian-jenkins
Yes I can go on call. Mr Vakharia does list rectovaginal and ureter and bowel. If you open the “information for professionals” section on his bupa page, it has these codes
There are also many other endo surgeons in london too that do complex cases including Mr Tom Holland, Mr Amer Raza etc
I would like to know, will my details or my phone number be shared with anyone from this AI chat?
Can you help me find the right specialist?

I’m seeking help with my endometriosis. I have endometriosis, thoracic endometriosis, adenomyosis, pelvic congestion syndrome, bowel prolapses and lichen sclerosus. I’m based in Suffolk but willing to travel anywhere in 120 miles. I’m self funding. I would like to look at private and NHS.

Hi Jack
1. Thoracic endometriosis is based on scans, pelvic is surgical. 
2. ⁠previous laparoscopic surgeries.
3. ⁠yea I have lots of chest symptoms, nose bleeds, chest pain, breathing difficulties, breathlessness, and wheezing, and these are cyclical. No lung collapses confirmed however they suspect I have had smaller lung collapses that don’t show on X-ray 
4. ⁠No hormonal treatment due to hormones making me unwell - migraines with aura and poor mental health.
5. ⁠Surgical goal I need a pelvic/bowel lap and need VATs for the lung side. No idea what is happening with my bowel prolapses, my uterus is pushing on my rectum and it looks fused so I imagine that will need managing

1. It was just a general surgeon, who was not a BSGE Surgeon. 
2. I have had a chest MRI which was reviewed by a thoracic specialist who noted lesions on the diaphragm (mostly chest side not abdomen side), pleura, segment 8 of my liver and lesions close to my IVC. Recommended both side VATS.
3. ⁠yes it's been confirmed by MRI, my uterus and right ovary are fused to my rectum, possible deep disease in bowel (I bleed rectally during periods also) my 2 rectal prolapses were picked up on a proctogram test. 
4. ⁠My colorectal team at my local hospital picked up 2 cases of bowel prolapse. One is called a rectal intussusception and the other is a rectocele- offered in help with it, I've just been left. No other input. 
Thank you x
That's great thank you for this information on surgeon. I wont see mabroak as I seen him before privately, and didn't have a great experience. But I can definitely look into the other surgeons

He told me he wouldn't operate on me unless I had a full hysterectomy. And then told me my lesions found on my liver and diaphragm were just shadows from my breast implants - took that same scan to my thoracic surgeon privately who completely disagreed with his opinion and he couldn't believe how he treated me and how he missed the thoracic endo so clearly. He reduced me to tears and tried to push me into a hysterectomy despite me telling him I didn't know if I had completed my family at that point. He is known for refusing to operate on some women in complex cases unfortunately. 
Thank you. I'm going to have a quick look at these surgeons x
Thank you for looking into this for me, very helpful. I will definitely look into this xx

hi there, it’s jess here doing some beta testing! but i would love to find:
- a specialist who works in nutrition for ibs, especially post infectious ibs triggered by antibiotics and food poisoning
- ⁠surbiton, london 
- ⁠no insurance 
thank you!

Hey, thanks for following up :)
I have seen a nutritionist in the past, this was around 1.5 years ago and I have been working alone since. I notice my symptoms are worse when I eat out of routine and drink alcohol. Online works for me!
thanks!
These all look great, thank you for sending them through!

From first glance, they look like they would all be a good fit. In the past I needed to set up intro calls to see if they were a personality match - it's often not their experience or knowledge that I'm unsure about, but whether they will be compatible on a social level! I think giving a clear ranking and explaining why they were most compatible would be handy. Also price is a big factor so I would find it really helpful if pricing was included, although I note 2/3 of them don't have pricing info aviaible online.

Often they have their own website which might have more info on pricing/packages available so it could be worth trying to find the nutritionists own website or socials for more detailed info. I would find it helpful for sure - I might actually find it more useful to have 5-10 links with less detailed information so I could browse a slightly wider range of professionals.
In terms of giving it the wow factor, if you were able to set up initial free consultation calls, or have a discount arranged for my first session, this is something that would set it apart and take some of the admin/financial burden away

if i think back to before i knew much about ibs and the gut system, the overview wouldn’t add much context as the terms wouldn’t really have meant anything to me. 
i can’t think of anything else off the top of my head at the moment but i think of anything i’ll send a message! 
no problem, happy to help ☺️

Can you help me find the right specialist?
An endometriosis specialist, in the West Mids UK and I have insurance (aviva)

I’ve had symptoms for over 10 years - extremely heavy and painful periods, migraines, painful sex, nausea, fatigue, pelvic pain, bloating. I’ve also started having bowel symptoms the last 6 months eg constipation to diarrhoea, small amounts of blood (not often), bowel/rectal pain, tummy cramps. 
I had excision surgery in June 2024 of superficial peritoneal endo on both pelvic sidewalls. Everything else was apparently fine however my symptoms did not change at all and have gotten worse since. I have had ultrasound and mri which also shows nothing apart from my uterus is tilting towards the right. No sign of adenomyosis etc but I have been on the pill for years which apparently can affect things so I am trying to investigate why surgery didn’t help my symptoms and if there is something else going on.

I’m not sure if it’s bsge-accredited but it was a private hospital - the Alexandra hospital, Cheadle. My surgeon was an endo specialist who has great experience with DIE and bowel endo - she said the bowels looked fine. Obviously there’s always a chance though, and she could have missed some! I didn’t have bowel symptoms at the time so she did not go into surgery expecting to find bowel endo. I’m not sure if that answers your questions enough!
I have had a referral to a gastro she works closely with but have not had the appointment yet. She did suggest a colonoscopy might be on the cards.

I’m on the combined oral contraceptive pill so don’t experience real periods. I have a withdrawal bleed every 3 months, I may have looser bowels in this time due to prostaglandins but experience the symptoms regardless of my cycle. This may be different if I wasn’t on the pill.
It feels like an extension - especially because I have sometimes experienced vaginal bleeding when the bowel symptoms are bad. It feels related - all symptoms flare up together.
I’m happy to travel a couple of hours but not for treatments like surgeries. For that I would prefer to stay in west mids.
I would receive treatment in the Manchester/Cheshire area where I had my last surgery.
Thank you
Thank you! I will look into them all individually, at a glance number 1 and 3 seem like the best fit. Or the one in Birmingham if I choose to have treatment closer to home
Lovely speaking with you both today! An Amazon voucher would be perfect, thank you 😊🙏🏽
Thank you so much!
